satire_attempt: Ah, the senior who fixes the bug in 30 minutes — clearly enlightened, definitely not just lucky that the bug happened to fall inside her muscle memory while the mid-level got assigned the genuinely novel problem. Next quarter Buddhi-Driven Engineering will be the LinkedIn post du jour, and a consulting firm will sell a workshop on it for $4,500.
verdict_reason: The satire rests on misreading the verse — the analog explicitly anticipates this in the counter-example: 'when the system is genuinely novel — a Postgres major-version upgrade with new locking semantics, an unfamiliar Kubernetes admission controller — skill-in-action does not mean speed.' The cynic's takedown requires ignoring this clause, which is hard because the engineering body draws a hard line: 'Skill-in-action does not mean speed. It means the quality of action issuing from the settled discriminating layer.' The artifact-density diagnostic across two months is also explicitly NOT 'who fixed the bug fastest'. The satire is forced; the engineering layer holds. Pass.
